propositions: canonical_tracker uses os.path.realpath to anchor the lock path so symlinked and real-path callers contend on the same lock, but no test references this invariant.
Add a test where holding the lock via the real path causes a second acquire via a symlink to raise LockTimeout, then succeed after release.
Add the reverse test where holding via the symlink blocks a real-path acquire with LockTimeout, then succeeds after release.
The tests are deterministic using timeout=1, attempts=1, relying on a genuinely-held lock rather than sleep timing.
If canonicalization were broken, the two paths would key different lock files and the second acquire would wrongly succeed, so the test exercises the invariant.
